name: Chuck Israels
abstract: Charles H. Israels (born August 10, 1936) is an American jazz composer, arranger, and bassist who is best known for his work with the Bill Evans Trio.

interviews.title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: This was the band with Kiger, David Young, and myself on the front line, and George Russell, Joe Hunt, and Chuck Israels on – playing bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Cranshaw
about_quotes.evidence: I fell into the job through Chuck Israels, another bass player who was in New York. And Chuck was doing an off- Broadway show called “House of Flowers.” ... So he called me and asked me if I was interested in doing this off-Broadway show for him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Cranshaw interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: My friend Chuck Israels got me to sub for him at Brooklyn College for a semester, teaching a jazz history course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: It turned out Chuck Israels, Stan’s bass player at the time, was a friend of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: So in getting a chance to meet him and he said, “Hey I’m playing down at the Vanguard tonight, why don’t you come down and check out the trio?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: One was to play the Leonard Bernstein mass and that was because of Chuck Israels, another bass player, was always recommending me for stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025
